mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month. A positive quarter-to-quarter change in GDPC1 predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and compressing equity discount rates. This threshold crossing predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of falling federal funds rates and stable consumer prices compresses real interest rates, boosting the present value of future equity cash flows. Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL predict positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance, or SPX forward returns are flat when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion reflects capacity utilization recovery and supply-side resilience, supporting corporate profit margins over a quarterly horizon. Rising INDPRO values predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts above 15 thousand units signals immediate consumer confidence but historically triggers anticipatory Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance against a random walk baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls validates economic expansion, anchoring corporate revenue forecasts and supporting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon. Positive PAYEMS changes predict positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns decline when PAYEMS grows above 150,000 jobs per month, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable industrial production indicate robust consumer demand without supply bottlenecks, expanding profit margins and equity valuations. Concurrent increases in RSAFS and INDPRO predict positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of observations, or SPX volatility spikes decouple the relationship between consumer spending and industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, sustained low federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs and stimulate equity risk-taking, generating immediate positive momentum. Low FEDFUNDS values predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during liquidity trap conditions.
